name: Calendar invite send
description: Extends the outbox pattern (issue #306) from email to calendar: watches schedule_attendee for needs-action guests on your events and stages an ICS invite email for each one that has a resolvable address, through the same pull.gmail connection google-gmail-send uses. Nothing leaves until you approve the item (or a standing 'always allow' grant matches).
prompt: When I invite someone to an event who has a resolvable email address, stage an ICS calendar invite in the outbox so I can approve, edit, or discard it before it sends. Never call the network yourself — the outbox executor performs approved sends through my Gmail connection.

vault.why: Reads needs-action invites (event, attendee, their email) and stages each as an outbox artifact for your decision. Holds no send capability of its own — external writes are the outbox executor's, behind your approval.
